metadata.title:
TUNNELSCHEME


STILL BURIED

IT APPEARS AGAIN AS AN
ANONYMOUS UNION DEPOT
PROJECT, BUT THE COUNCIL
WANTS ITS CHARACTER GER.
TlEiCATE.

pages.raw_text:
A union depot and tunnel COM
pany, unidentified. nameless and I'M
known, is backing the application of
]. A. Lynch for a tunnel around
through the hills south of town, &C.
cording to information" furnished
the city council last night by Attor-
new <. II. Belden. Notwithstanding
this startling revelation, the coun-
Gil will not give give the proposition
any further consideration until the
company is regularly organized and
shows a certificate of good charac-
ter.


Councilman Phillips proposed
that Promoter Lynch furnish a Sl0,-
600 bond to carry out the provisions
of the proposed franchise and At
torney peiden said he supposed his
client could do it," 1t is still be-
lieved that the tunnel is railroad
project.


Mr.. Belden stated after his ses-
sion with the council that this inn
preston had gotten out before and
he was quite willing to allow it to
be thought. hut now that he was
compelled to give away the facts,
he was sorry because it might eff
feet certain dealings. The council
decided to let the franchise remain
in committee of the whole until
the mystery of it is cleared up.


]. A. Lynch the promoter, owns
most of the property at each end of
the proposed tunnel. 1t is general
ly believed the scheme is to boost
the price of this real estate. 1t is
argued that if the scheme was bona
fide Lynch would accept the propo-
sition made by the council that he
give a $4,000 guarantee to com.
plete the work.
